sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.14 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: (C.\,C.\,Edmunds, G.\,Rosenberger). We call a~pair of natural numbers$(k,m)$admissible\/ if in the derived subgroup$F_2'$of a free group$F_2$there is an element$w$such that the commutator length of the element$w^m$is equal to$k$. Find all admissible pairs. It is known that every pair$(k, 2)$,$k 2$, is admissible (V.\,G.\,Bardakov, Algebra and Logic, 39 (2000), 224--251).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
